description_long: This object is a military jet aircraft with a sleek, angular fuselage and sharply pointed nose, painted in a matte gray color with red star insignias on the wings and tail, indicating a Soviet or Russian origin. The wings are mid-mounted and swept back, with additional small stabilizing surfaces near the rear. The tail section features twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer, forming an H-tail configuration. Two large jet engines are mounted at the rear, with visible exhaust nozzles. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage, enclosed with a transparent canopy, and appears to be designed for a single pilot. The overall design emphasizes speed and maneuverability, with minimal external protrusions and a streamlined profile.
description: A gray, swept-wing military jet with twin vertical stabilizers, red star insignias, and dual rear jet engines. The forward cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy.
description_view_0: Side view highlighting the pointed nose, forward cockpit, swept wings, and visible red star insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Rear oblique view showing the twin jet engine exhausts, H-tail configuration, and the angular shape of the wings and stabilizers.
description_view_2: Rear-side view emphasizing the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the placement of the engines at the back.
description_view_3: Top-rear view displaying the full wingspan, H-tail, and the streamlined, angular fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, swept-back wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines, rear-mounted in nacelles integrated with the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with main gear under the wings and a nose gear (not fully visible but implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy integrated into the fuselage.
